learningObjectives.title: Understand freight units
learningObjectives.description: Explain freight unit as the core transportation demand object in SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand freight unit lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Describe creation, planning, consolidation and conversion into freight orders.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate freight unit and freight order
learningObjectives.description: Clarify planning input versus execution output in transportation process.

sections.content: A Freight Unit (FU) is the primary transportation demand object in SAP TM. It represents the goods that must be transported from one location to another. Transportation planning begins with Freight Units, which are later consolidated into Freight Orders for execution.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Freight Units represent transportation demand. They are the primary input to the transportation planning process.

sections.title: Information Stored in a Freight Unit

sections.content: A manufacturing company creates ten outbound deliveries for customers in Bengaluru. SAP TM automatically generates ten Freight Units. The optimizer consolidates these Freight Units into three Freight Orders based on vehicle capacity, delivery schedule and transportation zone.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Keep Freight Units as granular transportation demands. Let the optimizer consolidate them automatically rather than creating oversized Freight Units manually.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A consumer goods company generated approximately 8,500 Freight Units every day. SAP TM consolidated them into 2,100 optimized Freight Orders, reducing transportation costs by nearly 20%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: One of the most common SAP TM interview questions is: 'What is a Freight Unit?' Always answer that it is the transportation demand object and the primary input for transportation planning.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Freight Units with Deliveries. Deliveries originate in ERP, while Freight Units are transportation planning objects created in SAP TM.

items: Freight Unit is the primary transportation demand object.
Freight Units are generated from ERP business documents.
Transportation planning starts with Freight Units.
Multiple Freight Units can be consolidated into one Freight Order.
Freight Units are planning objects, not execution documents.
